name: Jane Remover
description:
'''Jane Remover''' (born September 26, 2003) is an American rapper, producer, and singer-songwriter. They{{Efn|Remover is [[non-binary]]<ref name="NME2025">{{Cite web |last=Rigotti |first=Alex |date=June 24, 2025 |title=Rage and love: Why Jane Remover had to hit reset with ''Revengeseekerz'' |url=https://www.nme.com/features/music-interviews/jane-remover-revengeseekerz-interview-3872077 |access-date=June 24, 2025 |website=[[NME]] |archive-date=June 24, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250624104631/https://www.nme.com/features/music-interviews/jane-remover-revengeseekerz-interview-3872077 |url-status=live }}</ref> and goes by any pronouns, preferring ''[[Singular they|they/them]]''.<ref>{{Cite tweet |author=Jane Remover |user=janeremover |number=2018022506452402302 |date=1 February 2026 |title=I use any ✌️but I do prefer they |access-date=19 March 2026 |link=yes |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.is/ANrYG |archive-date=1 February 2026}}</ref>{{New archival link needed}} This article uses ''they/them'' for consistency.}} made their debut with ''[[Teen Week]]'' in 2021, a [[progressive pop]] and [[digicore]] EP with themes of [[adolescence]]. The same year, they released their debut studio album, ''[[Frailty (Jane Remover album)|Frailty]]'', a [[coming of age]] album following the similar themes of ''Teen Week'', but encompassing a wide range of genres. After coming out as [[transgender]], they released their second studio album, ''[[Census Designated]]'', in 2023, a [[shoegaze]] and [[post-rock]] [[concept album]] inspired by a "near-death experience" on a road trip. In 2025, they released their third studio album, ''[[Revengeseekerz]]'', with influence from digicore and [[Electronic dance music|EDM]].

In their side project '''Leroy''', they pioneered the "[[dariacore]]" [[microgenre]] on [[SoundCloud]] with their [[Dariacore (album)|album of the same name]], in 2021. They also have an [[indie rock]] side project '''Venturing''', with its most notable release being the album ''[[Ghostholding]]'' in 2025.
